headline: Pedestrian Deaths Reach 850 Amid Infrastructure Failures
context: MIROS recorded 850 pedestrian fatalities on Malaysian roads in 2025 with 72% in areas without proper sidewalks.

cards.big: 850 pedestrians killed in 2025 with 72% in areas lacking sidewalks
cards.sub: Pedestrian fatality rate 4 times higher than Singapore and 3 times South Korea

cards.big: Only 35% of urban roads in Malaysia have continuous pedestrian walkways
cards.sub: RM2.8 billion allocated for pedestrian infrastructure but only 40% disbursed

cards.big: 68% of pedestrian deaths involved victims over 60 years of age
cards.sub: Signal crossing times of 12 seconds insufficient for elderly to clear 4-lane roads

cards.big: JKR pedestrian guidelines updated 2024 but adoption is voluntary
cards.sub: Only Penang and KL made the new standards mandatory for road projects

cards.big: Pedestrian deaths test whether roads are designed for all users or only vehicles
850 pedestrian deaths indict the assumption that roads are built for cars, not the people beside them.
